primary_complaint: Worsening left hip pain, evaluate for surgical intervention
functional_limitations: Difficulty ambulating distances greater than 200 feet

denial_risk_flags.label: X-ray Results Pending at Submission
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart explicitly states 'X-ray left hip was ordered at today's visit - results pending' and notes 'PA submission requested in advance of imaging completion.' United Healthcare requires completed imaging results to establish medical necessity before authorizing CPT 27130.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Do not submit the prior authorization until the left hip X-ray results are documented in the chart. Add a chart addendum with the X-ray date, findings, and radiologist interpretation.

denial_risk_flags.label: Imaging Not Yet Complete
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Payers require completed imaging results before authorizing surgical procedures. Both the X-ray (ordered at 01/30/2025, results pending) and MRI (not yet ordered) are outstanding.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Do not submit until imaging results are available and documented.

denial_risk_flags.label: Insufficient Conservative Care
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Only 1 treatment documented (OTC pain medication with no duration). Payers for CPT 27130 typically require documented failure of physical therapy (minimum 6 weeks), NSAIDs, and at least one injection before approving surgical intervention. The chart explicitly notes 'No formal physical therapy documented in referral.'
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Obtain records documenting additional conservative treatments including formal physical therapy (minimum 6 weeks), prescription NSAIDs with duration, and corticosteroid or hyaluronic acid injections, or initiate and document further conservative care before submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Physical Therapy Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart explicitly states 'No formal physical therapy documented in referral.' United Healthcare typically requires a minimum of 6 weeks of supervised physical therapy as a prerequisite for total hip arthroplasty authorization.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Initiate and document a formal physical therapy program of at least 6 weeks duration, or obtain prior PT records if therapy was completed elsewhere, before submitting the prior authorization.

denial_risk_flags.label: OTC Medication — No Duration Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The only conservative treatment documented is 'over-the-counter pain medication at home' with no duration, dosage, or specific medication name. Payers require specific medication names, dosing, and duration to confirm adequate conservative care failure.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Addend the chart to specify the medication name (e.g., ibuprofen, naproxen), dosage, frequency, and duration of use, as well as the reason for discontinuation or failure.

denial_risk_flags.label: Preliminary Chart — Pre-op Workup Incomplete
denial_risk_flags.explanation: The chart states 'Chart is preliminary referral documentation. Full pre-op workup pending.' Submitting a prior authorization based on incomplete documentation increases the likelihood of denial due to insufficient medical necessity evidence.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Defer PA submission until the full pre-operative workup is complete, including imaging results, ASA classification, BMI, and a complete conservative care history.

denial_risk_flags.label: Symptom Duration Vague — No Specific Timeframe
denial_risk_flags.explanation: Symptom duration is documented only as 'over the past several months,' which is imprecise. Payers may require a specific duration to assess chronicity and medical necessity for surgical intervention.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Addend the chart to specify the exact onset date or number of months the patient has experienced left hip pain to support chronicity of the condition.

denial_risk_flags.label: No Injection Therapy Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: No corticosteroid or hyaluronic acid injections are documented. United Healthcare typically requires at least one intra-articular injection attempt before approving total hip arthroplasty (CPT 27130).
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Document whether injection therapy was attempted, refused, or contraindicated. If not yet attempted, consider administering and documenting an intra-articular corticosteroid injection prior to PA submission.

denial_risk_flags.label: No ASA Classification or BMI Documented
denial_risk_flags.explanation: ASA classification and BMI are absent from this chart. These are commonly required by United Healthcare for surgical authorization of CPT 27130 to assess surgical risk and appropriateness.
denial_risk_flags.recommendation: Add ASA classification and current BMI to the chart as part of the pre-operative assessment prior to PA submission.

diagnosis_codes.note: M16.12 (primary osteoarthritis, left hip) is a clinically appropriate and specific ICD-10 code that directly supports medical necessity for left total hip arthroplasty (CPT 27130).

conservative_treatments_named.note: Only 1 distinct conservative treatment modality documented (need ≥ 2).

conservative_treatment_duration.note: Only 1 duration-eligible conservative treatment documented (need ≥ 2 course-based treatments to evaluate duration; single-administration treatments like injections are excluded).

imaging_findings.note: No completed imaging with findings is documented.

surgical_approach.note: No surgical approach or technique is documented for the requested procedure.

cpt_code_valid.note: CPT 27130 is a recognized orthopedic surgical code.

symptom_duration.note: Symptom duration ("over the past several months") does not confirm ≥ 12 weeks.

hard_blocks.label: Imaging Findings
hard_blocks.message: No imaging results are documented. Payers require objective imaging evidence to authorize surgical intervention.

soft_warnings.label: Surgical Approach
soft_warnings.message: Anatomical approach details strengthen the surgical indication but can be inferred.
